name: SolarWinds Orion & SWIS REST API
what_it_does: SolarWinds Orion REST API (SWIS - SolarWinds Information Service) for IT infrastructure monitoring and operations platform. Enables AI agents to manage node and device monitoring configuration and thresholds, handle network performance and availability alert retrieval, access SNMP and WMI poll data for network and server metrics, retrieve NCM (Network Configuration Manager) device configuration backup and compliance data, manage alert acknowledgment and suppression workflows, handle IPAM integration and subnet tracking, access SRM (Storage Resource Monitor) and SAM (Server Application Monitor) data, retrieve historical performance trending and capacity planning data, manage topology discovery and network map data, and integrate SolarWinds monitoring data with ITSM, SIEM, and automation platforms.
use_cases: Retrieving network performance alerts from NOC automation agents
Managing device monitoring configuration from infrastructure agents
Accessing network topology data from asset management agents
Integrating monitoring alerts with ITSM from service management agents
not_for: Cloud-native APM without traditional on-premises infrastructure monitoring
Endpoint security without network and server performance monitoring
Consumer networking without enterprise NOC and ITOM capabilities
best_when: An enterprise using SolarWinds Orion wants AI agents to automate alert management, device configuration compliance, performance data retrieval, SNMP polling automation, and ITSM integration.
avoid_when: SECURITY CAUTION: SolarWinds was the target of the SUNBURST supply chain attack (2020) — SolarWinds has significantly improved security posture since, but maintain heightened monitoring of SolarWinds platform activity. Orion database (SWIS) queries can return large datasets — implement rate limiting and pagination for production automation.

auth.notes: SolarWinds SWIS uses HTTP Basic auth or Windows authentication. OrionSDK for Python and PowerShell automation. SWQL (SolarWinds Query Language) for data queries. REST API for CRUD operations. No native webhooks — alert escalation via ITSM integration. SolarWinds Service Desk (SWSD) has separate REST API with OAuth2.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per node/element licensing
pricing.paid_tiers: Perpetual per-node licensing; NPM, NCM, SAM, SRM modules separately priced; SolarWinds Observability SaaS available
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas. SolarWinds Corporation. NYSE: SWI. Founded 1999. IT monitoring market leader. 300,000+ customers. SUNBURST incident (Dec 2020) involved SolarWinds Orion update mechanism. SEC settlement 2023. Company has invested significantly in security posture post-incident. SolarWinds Observability SaaS platform as cloud-native successor.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits depend on Orion server hardware; SWQL queries can be resource-intensive

known_agent_gotchas: SECURITY CAUTION: Post-SUNBURST, SolarWinds has improved security but maintain heightened monitoring of Orion platform access and changes
SWQL query language — SolarWinds uses proprietary SWQL (similar to SQL) for data queries; learn SWQL before automating data extraction
On-premises deployment — SWIS API requires network access to Orion server management interface; no cloud endpoint for on-prem deployments
HTTP Basic auth — legacy auth mechanism; service account with least-privilege Orion role recommended for automation
Module-specific APIs — NPM, NCM, SAM, SRM have module-specific data entities; understand module schema before querying
SolarWinds Observability SaaS — cloud-native replacement has different REST API; if migrating, plan for API differences
agent_readiness.security_notes: IT infrastructure monitoring. SOC2, FedRAMP. Basic auth. On-premises. Network device, server, and application monitoring data.

security_score_components.security_notes: IT monitoring. SOC2, FedRAMP. Basic auth (legacy). On-premises. Infrastructure monitoring and network config data.
